user_message: Need help with Humongous Insurance -- run the hygiene check. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 11:45 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance security posture

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 13:33 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-03-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
